# UC-22.8.17 · SOC 2 CC6 — Physical Access Review Exception Tracking for Sensitive Facilities

## Description

CC6 includes physical protections. Correlating badge reader logs with quarterly access certification exceptions proves reviews occurred and lingering physical access was explicitly accepted or revoked.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes physical and sourcetypes pacs:access. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Map `badge_id` to human identity via secure lookup; (2) restrict site_id list to SOC-relevant facilities; (3) alert when access after `exp` without new cert row; (4) PII-minimize dashboards; (5) retain aligned with physical security policy (often 1 year). Indexes required: `index=physical`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pacs:access`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom HEC or CSV from PACS, Splunk DB Connect.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=physical` `sourcetype="pacs:access"` (badge_id, site_id, door_id, granted, _time); `physical_access_cert.csv` (badge_id, review_quarter, decision, reviewer, expires_on).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map `badge_id` to human identity via secure lookup; (2) restrict site_id list to SOC-relevant facilities; (3) alert when access after `exp` without new cert row; (4) PII-minimize dashboards; (5) retain aligned with physical security policy (often 1 year).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="pacs:access" granted=true earliest=-30d site_id IN ("DC1","DC2","HQ_SEC")
| stats latest(_time) as last_physical by badge_id, site_id
| inputlookup append=t physical_access_cert.csv
| stats latest(review_quarter) as rq, latest(decision) as dec, latest(expires_on) as exp, max(last_physical) as last_physical by badge_id, site_id
| eval exp_epoch=strptime(expires_on,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where isnull(exp_epoch) OR exp_epoch<now() OR isnull(dec)
| table badge_id, site_id, last_physical, rq, dec, exp
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 CC6 — Physical Access Review Exception Tracking for Sensitive Facilities** — CC6 includes physical protections. Correlating badge reader logs with quarterly access certification exceptions proves reviews occurred and lingering physical access was explicitly accepted or revoked.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=physical` `sourcetype="pacs:access"` (badge_id, site_id, door_id, granted, _time); `physical_access_cert.csv` (badge_id, review_quarter, decision, reviewer, expires_on). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom HEC or CSV from PACS, Splunk DB Connect. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: physical; **sourcetype**: pacs:access. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype="pacs:access", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **badge_id, site_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `stats` aggregates events by **badge_id, site_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **exp_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(exp_epoch) OR exp_epoch<now() OR isnull(dec)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: badge_id, dec, exp, exp_epoch, last_physical, rq, site_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the asset/access review evidence. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (exceptions), Map or bar by site_id, Single value (uncertified active badges).

## SPL

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="pacs:access" granted=true earliest=-30d site_id IN ("DC1","DC2","HQ_SEC")
| stats latest(_time) as last_physical by badge_id, site_id
| inputlookup append=t physical_access_cert.csv
| stats latest(review_quarter) as rq, latest(decision) as dec, latest(expires_on) as exp, max(last_physical) as last_physical by badge_id, site_id
| eval exp_epoch=strptime(expires_on,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where isnull(exp_epoch) OR exp_epoch<now() OR isnull(dec)
| table badge_id, site_id, last_physical, rq, dec, exp
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src span=1d | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (exceptions), Map or bar by site_id, Single value (uncertified active badges).
